title: Hook-based teammate load reporting, surfaced in the status-page viewer
description: A hook logs per-teammate activity so we can see which Teammates bear the most load, surfaced in the status-page viewer. Grounding: teammate-hook-triggers.md §0 (every tool event carries agent_type/agent_id; the §0 probe is a per-tool JSONL append — the telemetry mechanism), §1 (PostToolUse/PostToolBatch as the 'agent did X' signal), §2.3 (identity disambiguation). Spans two owners: hook wiring → builder (Template), reporting UI → viewer. Resolved at the sprint-4 grill: the record is hook-appended .excn/load_progress.json (the *_progress.json ignore class, ADR-0005), viewer-fetchable. Rides the EXEC-046 plumbing; ships disabled by default. Src-First.
acceptance_criteria: The hook appends per-tool-event load records to .excn/load_progress.json with identity from agent_type/agent_id
load_progress.json has a schema in .excn/schemas/ and stays in the *_progress.json ignore class
The status-page viewer renders a per-Teammate load lane from it
Ships disabled by default; enabling via the toggle config activates it
fix: Hook side: load-report.js + load-progress.schema.json (rolling 5000-record window, ships off). Viewer side: per-Teammate load panel in viewer/ (absent-file off-state, broken-file named error, agent_type/agent_id aggregation). Gates PASS (viewer lane after one revise cycle: ONE_MINUTE_MS).
notes: Filed 2026-06-10 (Brooks). Exploratory; acceptance criteria set by the EXEC-043 grill/PRD. Hook side and viewer lane both shipped + gated 2026-06-10.
